data.description:
## Description

**What**: Add an unacknowledged-degradation pre-dispatch check in sprint SKILL.md Step 10a that blocks story closure when unacked degradation entries exist in PRECONDITIONS.

**Why**: SC4 requires the sprint closure path to enforce the ack contract — a story with graceful-degradation fallthroughs that have not been acknowledged cannot be marked closed without evidence the team reviewed the fallthrough implications.

**Scope**:
- IN: New bash block in sprint SKILL.md Step 10a placed BEFORE the existing OPEN_CHILDREN check; reads PRECONDITIONS events for the story via _read_latest_preconditions; counts degradation:true entries without a paired ack event (matched by decision_id in ack event's decision_ids array — using S1 frozen ack format); blocks closure when count > 0; pre-manifest exemption (no PRECONDITIONS events for story → trivially passes); boundary documentation with dso-94ya (integrate into preflight harness if dso-94ya ships first, else ship standalone and dso-94ya integrates on landing)
- OUT: Ack mechanism implementation (S5); pre-commit hook (S4)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, a story with at least one unacked degradation entry cannot be transitioned to closed via the sprint workflow; the check produces an actionable error listing the unacked decision_ids
  ← Satisfies: "A story cannot transition to closed while any unacked degradation entry exists" (SC4)
- When this story is complete, a legacy story with no PRECONDITIONS events trivially passes the check (pre-manifest exemption)
  ← Satisfies: "Pre-manifest exemption: when _read_latest_preconditions returns no events...the check trivially passes" (SC4)
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Testing] Test must mock _read_latest_preconditions calls to the orphan branch; must not make live branch reads in test suite
- [Maintainability] S3 must use the EXACTLY frozen ack event format defined in S1 (fields: decision_ids, if_skipped, timestamp, sampled_set, schema_version) for ack-pairing logic; any deviation from S1's frozen format causes silent pairing failures (adversarial review finding)
- [Consumer Impact] Sprint Step 10a also dispatches dso:completion-verifier (per CLAUDE.md rule 20). Document the ordering: whether the unacked-degradation check runs BEFORE or AFTER completion-verifier dispatch. If before, completion-verifier sub-agent is never dispatched for stories with unacked degradations — this avoids wasted opus calls but must be explicitly documented in the implementation plan

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
data.title: As a sprint orchestrator, a story with unacknowledged degradation entries cannot be closed so graceful-degradation fallthroughs are always reviewed before story completion
